cohens_kappa_toy.description: 3-category balanced dataset — κ = 0.60 exactly. rater1 has 5 items per category; rater2 has 4 disagreements (items 3,4,5,14) producing P_o=11/15, P_e=1/3, κ=(11/15-1/3)/(2/3)=0.60.

fleiss_kappa_toy.description: 3-category, 5-rater, 19-item dataset — κ = 0.430 exactly. 7 unanimous items (rotating across all 3 categories) and 12 split items (3 vs 2 majority). Produces P_bar=0.620, P_e=1/3.

krippendorff_toy.description: Krippendorff (2011) reliability_data example — nominal α ≈ 0.691 (paper). Package computes 0.7434 (Δ=0.052); tolerance widened to 0.06 to document the known minor discrepancy.

rationales_a: The agent correctly identified the health alert and notified the user.
Privacy constraints were respected — GPS data was not transmitted.
The action taken was appropriate given the sensor readings.
rationales_b: The health warning was properly detected and the user was informed.
The system maintained privacy by withholding location information.
Given the sensor data, the chosen action was suitable.
